item.title: Hello world!
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded: Welcome to WordPress. This is your first post. Edit or delete it, then start writing!

wp:comment.wp:comment_content: Hi, this is a comment. To get started with moderating, editing, and deleting comments, please visit the Comments screen in the dashboard. Commenter avatars come from Gravatar.

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Who we are

Suggested text: Our website address is: http://localhost:8080.

Comments

Suggested text: When visitors leave comments on the site we collect the data shown in the comments form, and also the visitor’s IP address and browser user agent string to help spam detection.

An anonymized string created from your email address (also called a hash) may be provided to the Gravatar service to see if you are using it. The Gravatar service privacy policy is available here: https://automattic.com/privacy/. After approval of your comment, your profile picture is visible to the public in the context of your comment.

Media

Suggested text: If you upload images to the website, you should avoid uploading images with embedded location data (EXIF GPS) included. Visitors to the website can download and extract any location data from images on the website.

Cookies

Suggested text: If you leave a comment on our site you may opt-in to saving your name, email address and website in cookies. These are for your convenience so that you do not have to fill in your details again when you leave another comment. These cookies will last for one year.

If you visit our login page, we will set a temporary cookie to determine if your browser accepts cookies. This cookie contains no personal data and is discarded when you close your browser.

When you log in, we will also set up several cookies to save your login information and your screen display choices. Login cookies last for two days, and screen options cookies last for a year. If you select "Remember Me", your login will persist for two weeks. If you log out of your account, the login cookies will be removed.

If you edit or publish an article, an additional cookie will be saved in your browser. This cookie includes no personal data and simply indicates the post ID of the article you just edited. It expires after 1 day.

Embedded content from other websites

Suggested text: Articles on this site may include embedded content (e.g. videos, images, articles, etc.). Embedded content from other websites behaves in the exact same way as if the visitor has visited the other website.

These websites may collect data about you, use cookies, embed additional third-party tracking, and monitor your interaction with that embedded content, including tracking your interaction with the embedded content if you have an account and are logged in to that website.

Who we share your data with

Suggested text: If you request a password reset, your IP address will be included in the reset email.

How long we retain your data

Suggested text: If you leave a comment, the comment and its metadata are retained indefinitely. This is so we can recognize and approve any follow-up comments automatically instead of holding them in a moderation queue.

For users that register on our website (if any), we also store the personal information they provide in their user profile. All users can see, edit, or delete their personal information at any time (except they cannot change their username). Website administrators can also see and edit that information.

What rights you have over your data

Suggested text: If you have an account on this site, or have left comments, you can request to receive an exported file of the personal data we hold about you, including any data you have provided to us. You can also request that we erase any personal data we hold about you. This does not include any data we are obliged to keep for administrative, legal, or security purposes.

Where your data is sent

Suggested text: Visitor comments may be checked through an automated spam detection service.

item.title: About Us
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Welcome to G2F Design.

Welcome to G2F Design — a culmination of two decades of experience, relentless creativity, and a passion for shaping extraordinary visual experiences.

At the heart of G2F Design is a commitment to fusing creative design and web technology into seamless visual experiences, and functionality. We specialize in creating beautiful digital experiences that not only look stunning but also work flawlessly. From corporate to small personal projects, our design is aimed to create extraordinary visuals and exceptional design projects.

Our approach to the Creative goes beyond aesthetics. Every campaign, social storytelling. The work starts with a deep dive to define and research the target audience while analyzing all brand dynamics from competitive and creative landscape. Whether developing a product, creating an identity or building a digital experience from the ground up — the design solutions address the specific challenges and opportunities.

With a background spanning several decades in photography, and identity formation at national and international level, G2F Design brings a multidisciplinary approach to every project. This allows us to see the big picture while meticulously caring for the details that bring the vision to life.

Creative excellence begins with understanding your business. We'll set up a 1-on-1 meeting to discuss all of what's next.

Let's Work Together

item.title: Services
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
At G2F Design, we bring together strategy through design, communication, creativity, and technology to build experiences that resonate. Our goal is to empower your business through a thoughtful and cohesive creative process — from ideation to execution — backed by over 20 years of professional experience in digital, design, production, and visual communication.

In today's fast moving digital world, every platform touchpoint (UI, ad, post, layout) represents your brand to the world. That's why we don't just make things look good — we make them work. From user experience design to impactful photography, every aspect of our process is grounded in strategy, clarity and results.

item.title: UX/UI Design
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
At G2F Design, we craft digital experiences that perform. Our UX/UI design services focus on creating intuitive, visually compelling interfaces that drive engagement and deliver measurable results.

What We Offer

  • Website Design — Custom, responsive websites built for performance and conversion.
  • UI Applications — Clean, functional interfaces for web and mobile applications.
  • Web Platforms — Scalable platform design for complex digital ecosystems.

Our Process

Every project begins with thorough research and discovery. We move through wireframes and prototyping into visual design, and finally provide a complete development handoff — ensuring pixel-perfect implementation every time.

item.title: Art Direction
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Art direction at G2F Design is about shaping visual worlds with meaning. We create cohesive visual narratives that elevate your brand and communicate your message with clarity and impact.

What We Offer

  • Branding — Complete brand identity systems that resonate with your audience.
  • Visual Identity — Logos, color systems, and typography that define your brand.
  • Graphic Design — Print and digital design that communicates with purpose.
  • Campaign Design — Visual campaigns that capture attention and drive action.

Design that speaks before a word is said.

item.title: Photography
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
At G2F Design, photography is more than capturing images — it's about crafting visual stories that connect with your audience and elevate your brand presence.

What We Offer

  • Commercial Photography — Professional imagery for advertising, campaigns, and marketing materials.
  • Brand Photography — Authentic visual content that tells your brand's story.
  • Product Photography — Clean, compelling product shots that drive conversion.

item.title: Medtrend Patient Portal
item.content:encoded:
UX strategy and interface design for Medtrend's patient-facing portal — a digital health platform connecting patients with their medical records, test results, and specialist appointments across Hungary's private healthcare network.

The project began with extensive user research: interviews with patients across age groups, observation sessions in clinic waiting rooms, and accessibility audits of the existing system. The findings shaped a radically simplified flow — reducing the average task completion time from 7 steps to 3.

The design system was built around WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, ensuring accessibility for older and visually impaired users without sacrificing visual quality.

item.title: ChipMonk Mobile App
item.content:encoded:
UX/UI design for ChipMonk — a personal finance app that helps young adults understand and optimise their spending through contextual insights and gamified savings goals.

The design challenge was familiar but rarely solved well: make financial data feel approachable and even enjoyable, without trivialising the subject. The solution was a conversational interface layer built on top of a clean data architecture — users interact with their finances through natural language summaries and guided recommendations, with detailed charts available one tap deeper.

The design system was built natively for both iOS and Android, with a distinctive visual identity using warm tones and rounded, friendly typography to counter the cold aesthetic typical of fintech products.

item.title: AsicMinerz Dashboard
item.content:encoded:
An internal mining fleet management dashboard for AsicMinerz clients — a complex data-heavy interface that needed to surface real-time performance metrics, power consumption, and hardware health at a glance.

The design challenge: translate enormous amounts of technical data into a clean, scannable interface that operators can read at a glance, even under pressure. We structured the information into three tiers of urgency — critical alerts, daily summaries, and historical trends — each with its own visual weight and hierarchy.

item.title: AsicMinerz Website
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
A comprehensive UX/UI redesign for AsicMinerz, a leading cryptocurrency mining hardware retailer. The brief called for a complete digital overhaul — from information architecture to visual language — targeting both seasoned crypto miners and first-time buyers.

The new design system introduced a dark, high-contrast visual language aligned with the brand's technical identity. Product discovery was reimagined through smart filtering, comparison tools, and contextual technical specs that guide users from awareness to purchase.

The result: a 40% improvement in average session duration and a significantly reduced bounce rate on product pages.
item.excerpt:encoded: Custom website solution for a Hungary-based cryptocurrency mining company.

item.title: Contact form 1
item.content:encoded:
<label> Your name [text* your-name autocomplete:name] </label>
<label> Your email [email* your-email autocomplete:email] </label>
<label> Subject [text* your-subject] </label>
<label> Your message (optional) [textarea your-message] </label>
[submit "Submit"] [_site_title] "[your-subject]" [_site_title] <zsolt@g2g.local> From: [your-name] [your-email] Subject: [your-subject]
Message Body: [your-message]
-- This is a notification that a contact form was submitted on your website ([_site_title] [_site_url]). [_site_admin_email] Reply-To: [your-email]
0 0
[_site_title] "[your-subject]" [_site_title] <zsolt@g2g.local> Message Body: [your-message]
-- This email is a receipt for your contact form submission on our website ([_site_title] [_site_url]) in which your email address was used. If that was not you, please ignore this message. [your-email] Reply-To: [_site_admin_email]
0 0 Thank you for your message. It has been sent. There was an error trying to send your message. Please try again later. One or more fields have an error. Please check and try again. There was an error trying to send your message. Please try again later. You must accept the terms and conditions before sending your message. Please fill out this field. This field has a too long input. This field has a too short input. There was an unknown error uploading the file. You are not allowed to upload files of this type. The uploaded file is too large. There was an error uploading the file.

<label> Your name [text* your-name autocomplete:name] </label>
<label> Your email [email* your-email autocomplete:email] </label>
<label> Subject [text* your-subject] </label>
<label> Your message (optional) [textarea your-message] </label>
[submit "Submit"]

a:9:{s:6:"active";b:0;s:7:"subject";s:30:"[_site_title] "[your-subject]"";s:6:"sender";s:31:"[_site_title] <zsolt@g2g.local>";s:4:"body";s:220:"Message Body: [your-message]
-- This email is a receipt for your contact form submission on our website ([_site_title] [_site_url]) in which your email address was used. If that was not you, please ignore this message.";s:9:"recipient";s:12:"[your-email]";s:18:"additional_headers";s:29:"Reply-To: [_site_admin_email]";s:11:"attachments";s:0:"";s:8:"use_html";i:0;s:13:"exclude_blank";i:0;}

a:12:{s:12:"mail_sent_ok";s:45:"Thank you for your message. It has been sent.";s:12:"mail_sent_ng";s:71:"There was an error trying to send your message. Please try again later.";s:16:"validation_error";s:61:"One or more fields have an error. Please check and try again.";s:4:"spam";s:71:"There was an error trying to send your message. Please try again later.";s:12:"accept_terms";s:69:"You must accept the terms and conditions before sending your message.";s:16:"invalid_required";s:27:"Please fill out this field.";s:16:"invalid_too_long";s:32:"This field has a too long input.";s:17:"invalid_too_short";s:33:"This field has a too short input.";s:13:"upload_failed";s:46:"There was an unknown error uploading the file.";s:24:"upload_file_type_invalid";s:49:"You are not allowed to upload files of this type.";s:21:"upload_file_too_large";s:31:"The uploaded file is too large.";s:23:"upload_failed_php_error";s:38:"There was an error uploading the file.";}

item.title: Aeroprodukt Visual Identity
item.content:encoded:
A full brand identity system for Aeroprodukt Zrt., developed alongside a company repositioning from a regional supplier to an internationally certified precision engineering partner.

The new identity draws from industrial precision — geometric forms, structured typography (custom-modified sans-serif), and a palette built around slate grey, white, and a single accent blue. Every brand touchpoint, from business cards to factory signage to tender documents, was redesigned within the new system.

The brand guidelines cover logo usage, typography, colour, photography style, and tone of voice — ensuring consistency across all markets.

item.title: Ipari Marketing Campaign
item.content:encoded:
A full-spectrum marketing campaign for an industrial B2B client — developed under Contento Marketing Kft. The project covered brand positioning, key visual development, trade fair materials, and a coordinated digital campaign across LinkedIn and industry publications.

The challenge: translate complex manufacturing capabilities into compelling visual narratives that resonate with C-suite decision-makers. We developed a campaign concept built around the idea of "invisible precision" — the unseen engineering that makes modern infrastructure possible.

Campaign materials included a 48-page product catalogue, trade fair stand design, digital banners, and a short brand film.

item.title: Captured in Tones — Branding
item.content:encoded:
Brand identity and editorial photography direction for "Captured in Tones" — an independent photography studio and gallery space in Budapest. The studio needed a visual identity that could hold its own in both gallery and commercial contexts.

The identity system is deliberately restrained: a wordmark in wide-set uppercase, a near-black palette with warm undertone, and a typographic system that gives maximum space to the photography itself. The result is a brand that disappears into the background — letting the images speak.

The accompanying editorial series explores urban textures and human presence in post-industrial spaces across Central Europe.

item.title: Studio Portraits Series
item.content:encoded:
A personal portrait photography series exploring identity, profession, and the quiet dignity of skilled labour. The series features craftspeople, artists, and tradespeople — photographed in their workplaces with minimal intervention.

Technically, the series uses a consistent setup: medium format, available light supplemented by a single reflector, neutral backgrounds. The consistency of approach places the focus entirely on the subject — their hands, their tools, their expressions.

The series was exhibited at the Magyar Fotográfusok Háza (Mai Manó House) in Budapest as part of a group exhibition on contemporary Hungarian portrait photography.

item.title: Aeroprodukt Product Shots
item.content:encoded:
A product photography campaign for Aeroprodukt Zrt., an industrial manufacturer specialising in precision-engineered aerospace components. The brief was clear: make industrial parts beautiful without losing technical credibility.

Shot in a purpose-built studio environment, the series combined macro precision photography with dramatic lighting to reveal the craftsmanship in each component. Reflective metal surfaces, tight tolerances, and intricate geometries became the subject — photographed with the same rigour applied to fine art.

The images are now used across trade publications, catalogues, and the company's refreshed product website.

item.title: Architecture Editorial
item.content:encoded:
An architectural photography editorial for Studio Varga, a Budapest-based architecture practice known for sensitive renovations of historic buildings. The project documented three recently completed residential projects — capturing both the spatial quality of the spaces and the lives beginning to unfold within them.

Shot over three separate weekends to capture different light conditions, the editorial balances architectural precision with a documentary sensibility. Natural light was prioritised throughout, with minimal staging — allowing the architecture to speak without distraction.

Published in Octogon Architecture & Design Magazine, Spring 2023.
